    99→                if re.search(r'&\w+', stripped):
   100→                    raise ParseError("YAML anchors are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
   101→                if re.search(r'\*\w+', stripped):
   102→                    raise ParseError("YAML aliases are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
   103→
   104→        # Check for custom tags (anything that looks like !tag)
   105→        if re.search(r'!\w+', raw):
   106→            # But allow !!str, !!int etc (YAML builtin types)
   107→            lines = raw.split('\n')
   108→            for line in lines:
   109→                # Remove quoted strings
   110→                stripped = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '', line)
   111→                stripped = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", '', stripped)
   112→                # Check for custom tags (not !! builtin tags)
   113→                if re.search(r'(?<!!)!\w+', stripped):
   114→                    raise ParseError("YAML custom tags are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
   115→
   116→        result = yaml.load(raw, Loader=SafeYamlLoader)
   117→
   118→        # Check that all mapping keys are strings
   119→        def check_keys(obj):
   120→            if isinstance(obj, dict):
   121→                for k in obj.keys():
   122→                    if not isinstance(k, str):
   123→                        raise ParseError("YAML mapping keys must be strings", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
   124→                    check_keys(obj[k])
   125→            elif isinstance(obj, list):
   126→                for item in obj:
   127→                    check_keys(item)
   128→
   129→        check_keys(result)
   130→        return result
   131→    except yaml.YAMLError as e:
   132→        raise ParseError(f"Invalid YAML: {e}")
   133→
   134→
   135→def parse_toml(raw: str) -> Any:
   136→    """Parse a TOML string with strict rules (only JSON-representable values)."""
   137→    try:
   138→        result = toml.loads(raw)
   139→
   140→        # Check for non-JSON types
   141→        def check_json_types(obj, path=""):
   142→            if isinstance(obj, dict):
   143→                for k, v in obj.items():
   144→                    check_json_types(v, f"{path}/{k}")
   145→            elif isinstance(obj, list):
   146→                for i, item in enumerate(obj):
   147→                    check_json_types(item, f"{path}/{i}")
   148→            # Check for datetime objects
   149→            elif hasattr(obj, 'isoformat'):
   150→                raise ParseError(f"Non-JSON type at {path}: datetime not allowed", "non_json_type")
   151→
   152→        check_json_types(result)
   153→        return result
   154→    except toml.TomlDecodeError as e:
   155→        raise ParseError(f"Invalid TOML: {e}")
   156→
   157→
   158→def parse_raw_config(raw: str, fmt: str) -> Any:
   159→    fmt = fmt.lower()
   160→    parsers = {'json': parse_json, 'yaml': parse_yaml, 'toml': parse_toml}
   161→    if fmt not in parsers:
   162→        raise ParseError(f"Unsupported format: {fmt}")
   163→    return parsers[fmt](raw)
   164→
   165→
   166→# ============================================================================
   167→# JSON Schema Validation
   168→# ============================================================================
   169→
   170→class SchemaValidationError(Exception):
   171→    """Error during schema validation."""
   172→    def __init__(self, path: str, rule: str, expected: str, actual: str):
   173→        self.path = path
   174→        self.rule = rule
   175→        self.expected = expected
   176→        self.actual = actual
   177→        super().__init__(f"Validation failed at {path}")
   178→
   179→
   180→def get_json_type(value: Any) -> str:
   181→    """Get the JSON type of a value."""
   182→    if value is None:
   183→        return "null"
   184→    elif isinstance(value, bool):
   185→        return "boolean"
   186→    elif isinstance(value, int):
   187→        return "integer"
   188→    elif isinstance(value, float):
   189→        return "number"
   190→    elif isinstance(value, str):
   191→        return "string"
   192→    elif isinstance(value, list):
   193→        return "array"
   194→    elif isinstance(value, dict):
   195→        return "object"
   196→    return "unknown"
   197→
   198→
   199→class ExternalRefError(Exception):
   200→    pass
   201→
   202→
   203→def check_external_refs(obj):
   204→    """Raise ExternalRefError if obj contains any external $ref."""
   205→    if isinstance(obj, dict):
   206→        if '$ref' in obj:
   207→            ref = obj['$ref']
   208→            if ref.startswith(('http://', 'https://', '//')):
   209→                raise ExternalRefError()
   210→            if not ref.startswith('#') and ':' in ref.split('/')[0]:
   211→                raise ExternalRefError()
   212→        for v in obj.values():
   213→            check_external_refs(v)
   214→    elif isinstance(obj, list):
   215→        for item in obj:
   216→            check_external_refs(item)
   217→
   218→
   219→def validate_config_against_schema(config: Any, schema: Dict) -> None:
   220→    """Validate a config against a JSON Schema, raising SchemaValidationError on failure."""
   221→    try:
   222→        check_external_refs(schema)
   223→        validate(instance=config, schema=schema)
   224→    except jsonschema.exceptions.ValidationError as e:
   225→        # Extract path as JSON Pointer
   226→        path = "/" + "/".join(str(p) for p in e.absolute_path) if e.absolute_path else "/"
   227→
   228→        # Determine the rule and expected/actual values
   229→        rule = e.validator
   230→        expected = ""
   231→        actual = get_json_type(e.instance)
   232→
   233→        if rule == "type":
   234→            expected = e.validator_value
   235→            if isinstance(expected, list):
   236→                expected = ", ".join(expected)
   237→        elif rule == "enum":
   238→            expected = ", ".join(repr(v) for v in e.validator_value)
   239→        elif rule == "required":
   240→            path = path + "/" + e.validator_value[0] if e.validator_value else path
   241→            expected = "property required"
   242→            actual = "missing"
   243→        elif rule == "pattern":
   244→            expected = f"matching pattern {e.validator_value}"
   245→        elif rule == "minimum":
   246→            expected = f">= {e.validator_value}"
   247→            actual = str(e.instance)
   248→        elif rule == "maximum":
   249→            expected = f"<= {e.validator_value}"
   250→            actual = str(e.instance)
   251→        elif rule == "minLength":
   252→            expected = f"length >= {e.validator_value}"
   253→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   254→        elif rule == "maxLength":
   255→            expected = f"length <= {e.validator_value}"
   256→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   257→        elif rule == "minItems":
   258→            expected = f"items >= {e.validator_value}"
   259→            actual = f"items {len(e.instance)}"
   260→        elif rule == "maxItems":
   261→            expected = f"items <= {e.validator_value}"
   262→            actual = f"items {len(e.instance)}"
   263→        else:
   264→            expected = str(e.validator_value)
   265→            actual = str(e.instance)[:50]
   266→
   267→        raise SchemaValidationError(path, rule, expected, actual)
   268→    except ExternalRefError:
   269→        raise SchemaValidationError("/", "schema", "no external $ref", "external $ref found")
   270→    except Exception as e:
   271→        raise SchemaValidationError("/", "schema", "valid schema", str(e))
   272→
   273→
   274→# ============================================================================
   275→# Data Storage
   276→# ============================================================================
   277→
   278→class SchemaRegistry:
   279→    """Registry for JSON schemas with versioning."""
   280→
   281→    def __init__(self):
   282→        # schema_name -> {version -> schema}
   283→        self.schemas: Dict[str, Dict[int, Dict]] = {}
   284→
   285→    def create(self, name: str, schema: Dict) -> int:
   286→        """Create a new schema version, returning the version number."""
   287→        if name not in self.schemas:
   288→            self.schemas[name] = {}
   289→
   290→        versions = self.schemas[name]
   291→        if len(versions) >= 1000:
   292→            raise ValueError("Maximum schema versions exceeded")
   293→
   294→        new_version = max(versions.keys(), default=0) + 1
   295→        versions[new_version] = deepcopy(schema)
   296→        return new_version
   297→
   298→    def get(self, name: str, version: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
   299→        """Get a specific schema version."""
   300→        if name not in self.schemas:
   301→            return None
   302→        return self.schemas[name].get(version)
   303→
   304→    def list_versions(self, name: str) -> List[int]:
   305→        """List all versions of a schema."""
   306→        if name not in self.schemas:
   307→            return []
   308→        return sorted(self.schemas[name].keys())
   309→
   310→    def exists(self, name: str, version: int) -> bool:
   311→        """Check if a schema version exists."""
   312→        return name in self.schemas and version in self.schemas[name]
   313→
   314→
   315→class ConfigStore:
   316→    """Store for configurations with versioning."""
   317→
   318→    def __init__(self):
   319→        # (name, scope_key) -> {version -> config_entry}
   320→        self.configs: Dict[Tuple[str, str], Dict[int, Dict]] = {}
   321→        # (name, scope_key) -> binding_info
   322→        self.bindings: Dict[Tuple[str, str], Dict] = {}
   323→
   324→    @staticmethod
   325→    def scope_to_key(scope: Dict) -> str:
   326→        """Convert a scope dict to a canonical string key."""
   327→        return json.dumps(sorted(scope.items()))
   328→
   329→    @staticmethod
   330→    def key_to_scope(key: str) -> Dict:
   331→        """Convert a canonical string key back to a scope dict."""
   332→        return dict(json.loads(key))
   333→
   334→    def create_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict, config: Dict,
   335→                       includes: List[Dict] = None, schema_ref: Dict = None) -> int:
   336→        """Create a new config version."""
   337→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   338→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   339→
   340→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   341→            self.configs[full_key] = {}
   342→
   343→        versions = self.configs[full_key]
   344→        new_version = max(versions.keys(), default=0) + 1
   345→
   346→        entry = {
   347→            "config": normalize_value(deepcopy(config)),
   348→            "includes": includes or [],
   349→            "schema_ref": schema_ref
   350→        }
   351→        versions[new_version] = entry
   352→        return new_version
   353→
   354→    def get_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
   355→        """Get a specific config version."""
   356→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   357→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   358→
   359→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   360→            return None
   361→        return self.configs[full_key].get(version)
   362→
   363→    def get_latest_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict) -> Optional[int]:
   364→        """Get the latest version number for a config."""
   365→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   366→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   367→
   368→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   369→            return None
   370→        versions = self.configs[full_key]
   371→        if not versions:
   372→            return None
   373→        return max(versions.keys())
   374→
   375→    def get_config_entry(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int = None) -> Optional[Dict]:
   376→        """Get a config entry, optionally at a specific version."""
   377→        if version is None:
   378→            version = self.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   379→            if version is None:
   380→                return None
   381→        return self.get_version(name, scope, version)
   382→
   383→    def set_binding(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict) -> Dict:
   384→        """Set a schema binding for a config identity."""
   385→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   386→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   387→
   388→        binding = {
   389→            "name": name,
   390→            "scope": deepcopy(scope),
   391→            "schema_ref": deepcopy(schema_ref),
   392→            "active": True
   393→        }
   394→        self.bindings[full_key] = binding
   395→        return binding
   396→
   397→    def get_binding(self, name: str, scope: Dict) -> Optional[Dict]:
   398→        """Get the schema binding for a config identity."""
   399→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   400→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   401→        return self.bindings.get(full_key)
   402→
   403→    def resolve_config(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int = None,
   404→                       visited: set = None) -> Tuple[Dict, List[Dict]]:
   405→        """Resolve a config with includes, returning (resolved_config, inheritance_chain)."""
   406→        if visited is None:
   407→            visited = set()
   408→
   409→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   410→        visit_key = (name, scope_key, version)
   411→        if visit_key in visited:
   412→            raise ValueError(f"Circular include detected: {name}")
   413→        visited.add(visit_key)
   414→
   415→        entry = self.get_config_entry(name, scope, version)
   416→        if entry is None:
   417→            raise ValueError(f"Config not found: {name}")
   418→
   419→        config = deepcopy(entry["config"])
   420→        includes = entry.get("includes", [])
   421→
   422→        inheritance_chain = [{
   423→            "name": name,
   424→            "scope": deepcopy(scope),
   425→            "version": version or self.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   426→        }]
   427→
   428→        # Process includes in order (first one wins)
   429→        merged = {}
   430→        for inc in includes:
   431→            inc_name = inc["name"]
   432→            inc_scope = inc.get("scope", {})
   433→            inc_version = inc.get("version")
   434→
   435→            # Merge scopes: included config's scope + override from include directive
   436→            merged_scope = deepcopy(scope)
   437→            merged_scope.update(inc_scope)
   438→
   439→            inc_config, inc_chain = self.resolve_config(
   440→                inc_name, merged_scope, inc_version, visited.copy()
   441→            )
   442→            inheritance_chain.extend(inc_chain)
   443→
   444→            # Merge: included config values (lower priority)
   445→            merged = deep_merge(merged, inc_config)
   446→
   447→        # Current config values (higher priority)
   448→        merged = deep_merge(merged, config)
   449→
   450→        return merged, inheritance_chain
   451→
   452→
   453→def deep_merge(base: Dict, override: Dict) -> Dict:
   454→    """Deep merge two dictionaries, with override values taking precedence."""
   455→    result = deepcopy(base)
   456→    for key, value in override.items():
   457→        if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
   458→            result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
   459→        else:
   460→            result[key] = deepcopy(value)
   461→    return result
   462→
   463→
   464→# ============================================================================
   465→# HTTP Server
   466→# ============================================================================
   467→
   468→class ConfigServerHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
   469→    """HTTP request handler for the config server."""
   470→
   471→    schemas: SchemaRegistry = None
   472→    configs: ConfigStore = None
   473→
   474→    def log_message(self, format, *args):
   475→        pass
   476→
   477→    def send_json_response(self, status: int, data: Any):
   478→        body = json.dumps(data, separators=(',', ':')).encode('utf-8')
   479→        self.send_response(status)
   480→        self.send_header('Content-Type', 'application/json; charset=utf-8')
   481→        self.send_header('Content-Length', len(body))
   482→        self.end_headers()
   483→        self.wfile.write(body)
   484→
   485→    def send_error_response(self, status: int, code: str, message: str, details: Dict = None):
   486→        error = {"code": code, "message": message}
   487→        if details:
   488→            error["details"] = details
   489→        self.send_json_response(status, {"error": error})
   490→
   491→    def send_validation_error(self, e: SchemaValidationError):
   492→        self.send_error_response(422, "validation_failed",
   493→                                "Config does not conform to schema", {
   494→                                    "path": e.path,
   495→                                    "rule": e.rule,
   496→                                    "expected": e.expected,
   497→                                    "actual": e.actual
   498→                                })
   499→
   500→    def resolve_effective_schema(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict = None):
   501→        """Return (effective_schema_ref, effective_schema) or (None, None) if sent error."""
   502→        if schema_ref:
   503→            schema_name = schema_ref.get("name")
   504→            schema_version = schema_ref.get("version")
   505→            if not self.schemas.exists(schema_name, schema_version):
   506→                self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Schema version not found")
   507→                return None, None
   508→            return schema_ref, self.schemas.get(schema_name, schema_version)
   509→        binding = self.configs.get_binding(name, scope)
   510→        if binding:
   511→            ref = binding["schema_ref"]
   512→            return ref, self.schemas.get(ref.get("name"), ref.get("version"))
   513→        return None, None
   514→
   515→    def read_request_body(self) -> bytes:
   516→        """Read the request body."""
   517→        content_length = int(self.headers.get('Content-Length', 0))
   518→        if content_length == 0:
   519→            return b'{}'
   520→
   521→        # Check size limit (1 MiB)
   522→        if content_length > 1024 * 1024:
   523→            return None
   524→
   525→        return self.rfile.read(content_length)
   526→
   527→    def parse_json_body(self) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[str]]:
   528→        """Parse the request body as JSON."""
   529→        body = self.read_request_body()
   530→        if body is None:
   531→            return None, "too_large"
   532→
   533→        try:
   534→            return json.loads(body.decode('utf-8')), None
   535→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   536→            return None, "invalid_json"
   537→
   538→    def parse_path(self) -> Tuple[str, List[str], Dict]:
   539→        """Parse the request path into components."""
   540→        parsed = urlparse(self.path)
   541→        path_parts = [p for p in parsed.path.split('/') if p]
   542→        query_params = parse_qs(parsed.query)
   543→        return parsed.path, path_parts, query_params
   544→
   545→    def do_POST(self):
   546→        """Handle POST requests."""
   547→        path, parts, query = self.parse_path()
   548→
   549→        # Validate content type
   550→        content_type = self.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
   551→        if not content_type.startswith('application/json'):
   552→            self.send_error_response(415, "unsupported_media_type",
   553→                                    "Content-Type must be application/json")
   554→            return
   555→
   556→        # Parse body
   557→        body, error = self.parse_json_body()
   558→        if error == "too_large":
   559→            self.send_error_response(413, "too_large", "Request body too large")
   560→            return
   561→        if error == "invalid_json":
   562→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid JSON body")
   563→            return
   564→
   565→        if body is None:
   566→            body = {}
   567→
   568→        # Route the request
   569→        try:
   570→            self.route_post(path, parts, body)
   571→        except Exception as e:
   572→            self.send_error_response(500, "internal_error", str(e))
   573→
   574→    def route_post(self, path: str, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   575→        if len(parts) < 3 or parts[0] != 'v1':
   576→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   577→            return
   578→
   579→        if parts[1] == 'schemas':
   580→            self._route_schema(parts, body)
   581→        elif parts[1] == 'configs':
   582→            self._route_config(parts, body)
   583→        else:
   584→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   585→
   586→    def _route_schema(self, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   587→        if len(parts) < 3:
   588→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   589→            return
   590→        schema_name = parts[2]
   591→
   592→        if len(parts) == 3:
   593→            self.handle_create_schema(schema_name, body)
   594→        elif len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == 'versions':
   595→            self.handle_list_schema_versions(schema_name, body)
   596→        elif len(parts) == 4:
   597→            try:
   598→                version = int(parts[3])
   599→                self.handle_get_schema_version(schema_name, version, body)
   600→            except ValueError:
   601→                self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid version number")
   602→        else:
   603→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   604→
   605→    def _route_config(self, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   606→        if len(parts) < 3:
   607→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   608→            return
   609→        name = parts[2]
   610→
   611→        # Handle action suffixes
   612→        if name.endswith(':bind'):
   613→            self.handle_bind_schema(name[:-5], body)
   614→        elif name.endswith(':resolve'):
   615→            self.handle_resolve_config(name[:-8], body)
   616→        elif name.endswith(':validate'):
   617→            self.handle_validate_config(name[:-9], body)
   618→        elif len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == 'schema':
   619→            self.handle_get_binding(name, body)
   620→        elif len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == 'versions':
   621→            self.handle_list_config_versions(name, body)
   622→        elif len(parts) == 4:
   623→            try:
   624→                version = int(parts[3])
   625→                self.handle_get_config_version(name, version, body)
   626→            except ValueError:
   627→                self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid version number")
   628→        elif len(parts) == 3:
   629→            self.handle_create_config(name, body)
   630→        else:
   631→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   632→
   633→    # ========================================================================
   634→    # Schema Handlers
   635→    # ========================================================================
   636→
   637→    def handle_create_schema(self, schema_name: str, body: Dict):
   638→        """Handle POST /v1/schemas/{schema_name}"""
   639→        schema = None
   640→
   641→        if "schema" in body:
   642→            # Structured JSON Schema
   643→            schema = body["schema"]
   644→        elif "raw_schema" in body:
   645→            # Raw schema
   646→            raw = body["raw_schema"]
   647→            fmt = body.get("raw_format", "json").lower()
   648→
   649→            if fmt not in ("json", "yaml"):
   650→                self.send_error_response(415, "unsupported_format",
   651→                                        f"Unsupported format: {fmt}")
   652→                return
   653→
   654→            # Check size
   655→            if len(raw.encode('utf-8')) > 1024 * 1024:
   656→                self.send_error_response(413, "too_large", "Schema too large")
   657→                return
   658→
   659→            try:
   660→                parsed = parse_raw_config(raw, fmt)
   661→                if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
   662→                    self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid",
   663→                                            "Schema must be an object")
   664→                    return
   665→                schema = parsed
   666→            except ParseError as e:
   667→                self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid", str(e),
   668→                                        {"reason": e.reason} if e.reason else None)
   669→                return
   670→        else:

   879→            )
   880→        except ValueError as e:

   906→    def handle_validate_config(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   907→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   908→        version = body.get("version")
   909→        schema_ref = body.get("schema_ref")
   910→        mode = body.get("mode", "resolved")
   911→
   912→        if mode not in ("stored", "resolved"):
   913→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid mode")
   914→            return
   915→
   916→        latest = self.configs.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   917→        if latest is None:
   918→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config not found")
   919→            return
   920→
   921→        version_used = version or latest
   922→
   923→        entry = self.configs.get_version(name, scope, version_used)
   924→        if entry is None:
   925→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config version not found")
   926→            return
   927→
   928→        effective_schema_ref, effective_schema = self.resolve_effective_schema(name, scope, schema_ref)
   929→        if effective_schema_ref is None and schema_ref is not None:
   930→            return  # Error already sent
   931→
   932→        if effective_schema is None:
   933→            self.send_error_response(404, "schema_not_bound", "No schema bound")
   934→            return
   935→
   936→        if mode == "stored":
   937→            config_to_validate = entry["config"]
   938→        else:
   939→            try:
   940→                config_to_validate, _ = self.configs.resolve_config(name, scope, version_used)
   941→            except ValueError as e:
   942→                self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", str(e))
   943→                return
   944→
   945→        try:
   946→            validate_config_against_schema(config_to_validate, effective_schema)
   947→            self.send_json_response(200, {
   948→                "name": name,
   949→                "scope": scope,
   950→                "version_used": version_used,
   951→                "mode": mode,
   952→                "valid": True,
   953→                "validated_against": effective_schema_ref
   954→            })
   955→        except SchemaValidationError as e:
   956→            self.send_validation_error(e)
   957→
   958→
   959→def run_server(address: str = "0.0.0.0", port: int = 8080):
   960→    """Run the config server."""
   961→    # Initialize storage
   962→    ConfigServerHandler.schemas = SchemaRegistry()
   963→    ConfigServerHandler.configs = ConfigStore()
   964→
   965→    server = HTTPServer((address, port), ConfigServerHandler)
   966→    print(f"Config server running on {address}:{port}", file=sys.stderr)
   967→
   968→    try:
   969→        server.serve_forever()
   970→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   971→        print("\nShutting down...", file=sys.stderr)
   972→        server.shutdown()
   973→
   974→
   975→def main():
   976→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Config Server")
   977→    parser.add_argument("--address", default="0.0.0.0", help="Address to bind to")
   978→    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8080, help="Port to bind to")
   979→    args = parser.parse_args()
   980→
   981→    run_server(args.address, args.port)
   982→
   983→
   984→if __name__ == "__main__":
   985→    main()
   986→

   161→class SchemaValidationError(Exception):
   162→    """Error during schema validation."""
   163→    def __init__(self, path: str, rule: str, expected: str, actual: str):
   164→        self.path = path
   165→        self.rule = rule
   166→        self.expected = expected
   167→        self.actual = actual
   168→        super().__init__(f"Validation failed at {path}")
   169→
   170→
   171→def get_json_type(value: Any) -> str:
   172→    """Get the JSON type of a value."""
   173→    if value is None:
   174→        return "null"
   175→    elif isinstance(value, bool):
   176→        return "boolean"
   177→    elif isinstance(value, int):
   178→        return "integer"
   179→    elif isinstance(value, float):
   180→        return "number"
   181→    elif isinstance(value, str):
   182→        return "string"
   183→    elif isinstance(value, list):
   184→        return "array"
   185→    elif isinstance(value, dict):
   186→        return "object"
   187→    return "unknown"
   188→
   189→
   190→class ExternalRefError(Exception):
   191→    pass
   192→
   193→
   194→def check_external_refs(obj):
   195→    """Raise ExternalRefError if obj contains any external $ref."""
   196→    if isinstance(obj, dict):
   197→        if '$ref' in obj:
   198→            ref = obj['$ref']
   199→            if ref.startswith(('http://', 'https://', '//')):
   200→                raise ExternalRefError()
   201→            if not ref.startswith('#') and ':' in ref.split('/')[0]:
   202→                raise ExternalRefError()
   203→        for v in obj.values():
   204→            check_external_refs(v)
   205→    elif isinstance(obj, list):
   206→        for item in obj:
   207→            check_external_refs(item)
   208→
   209→
   210→def validate_config_against_schema(config: Any, schema: Dict) -> None:
   211→    """Validate a config against a JSON Schema, raising SchemaValidationError on failure."""
   212→    try:
   213→        check_external_refs(schema)
   214→        validate(instance=config, schema=schema)
   215→    except jsonschema.exceptions.ValidationError as e:
   216→        # Extract path as JSON Pointer
   217→        path = "/" + "/".join(str(p) for p in e.absolute_path) if e.absolute_path else "/"
   218→
   219→        # Determine the rule and expected/actual values
   220→        rule = e.validator
   221→        expected = ""
   222→        actual = get_json_type(e.instance)
   223→
   224→        if rule == "type":
   225→            expected = e.validator_value
   226→            if isinstance(expected, list):
   227→                expected = ", ".join(expected)
   228→        elif rule == "enum":
   229→            expected = ", ".join(repr(v) for v in e.validator_value)
   230→        elif rule == "required":
   231→            path = path + "/" + e.validator_value[0] if e.validator_value else path
   232→            expected = "property required"
   233→            actual = "missing"
   234→        elif rule == "pattern":
   235→            expected = f"matching pattern {e.validator_value}"
   236→        elif rule == "minimum":
   237→            expected = f">= {e.validator_value}"
   238→            actual = str(e.instance)
   239→        elif rule == "maximum":
   240→            expected = f"<= {e.validator_value}"
   241→            actual = str(e.instance)
   242→        elif rule == "minLength":
   243→            expected = f"length >= {e.validator_value}"
   244→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   245→        elif rule == "maxLength":
   246→            expected = f"length <= {e.validator_value}"
   247→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   248→        elif rule == "minItems":
   249→            expected = f"items >= {e.validator_value}"

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Config Server - A configuration management service with schema registry support.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→import argparse
     7→import json
     8→import re
     9→import sys
    10→from copy import deepcopy
    11→from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
    12→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
    13→from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs
    14→import jsonschema
    15→from jsonschema import validate
    16→import yaml
    17→import toml
    18→
    19→
    20→def normalize_value(value: Any) -> Any:
    21→    """Normalize a JSON value to canonical form with sorted keys."""
    22→    if isinstance(value, dict):
    23→        return {k: normalize_value(v) for k, v in sorted(value.items())}
    24→    if isinstance(value, list):
    25→        return [normalize_value(item) for item in value]
    26→    if isinstance(value, float) and value == int(value) and abs(value) < 10**15:
    27→        return float(int(value))
    28→    return value
    29→
    30→
    31→class ParseError(Exception):
    32→    """Error parsing raw config."""
    33→    def __init__(self, message: str, reason: str = None):
    34→        super().__init__(message)
    35→        self.reason = reason
    36→
    37→
    38→def parse_json(raw: str) -> Any:
    39→    """Parse a JSON string with strict rules (no comments, no trailing commas)."""
    40→    try:
    41→        return json.loads(raw)
    42→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    43→        raise ParseError(f"Invalid JSON: {e}")
    44→
    45→
    46→class SafeYamlLoader(yaml.SafeLoader):
    47→    """Custom YAML loader that disallows anchors, aliases, custom tags, and merge keys."""
    48→    pass
    49→
    50→
    51→def yaml_construct_undefined(loader, node):
    52→    raise ParseError("YAML custom tags are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    53→
    54→
    55→def yaml_construct_merge(loader, node):
    56→    raise ParseError("YAML merge keys are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    57→
    58→
    59→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor(None, yaml_construct_undefined)
    60→# Re-add default constructors for standard types
    61→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:null', lambda l, n: None)
    62→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:bool', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_bool(n))
    63→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:int', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_int(n))
    64→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:float', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_float(n))
    65→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:str', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_str(n))
    66→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:seq', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_seq(n))
    67→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:map', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_map(n))
    68→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:binary', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_binary(n))
    69→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:timestamp', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_timestamp(n))
    70→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:omap', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_omap(n))
    71→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:pairs', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_pairs(n))
    72→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:set', lambda l, n: l.construct_yaml_set(n))
    73→
    74→
    75→def parse_yaml(raw: str) -> Any:
    76→    """Parse a YAML string with strict rules."""
    77→    try:
    78→        # Check for merge keys manually since they use a special << key
    79→        if '<<:' in raw or '<< :' in raw:
    80→            raise ParseError("YAML merge keys are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    81→
    82→        # Check for anchors and aliases
    83→        if '&' in raw or '*' in raw:
    84→            # More careful check - could be in strings
    85→            lines = raw.split('\n')
    86→            for line in lines:
    87→                # Remove quoted strings for checking
    88→                stripped = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '', line)
    89→                stripped = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", '', stripped)
    90→                if re.search(r'&\w+', stripped):
    91→                    raise ParseError("YAML anchors are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    92→                if re.search(r'\*\w+', stripped):
    93→                    raise ParseError("YAML aliases are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    94→
    95→        # Check for custom tags (anything that looks like !tag)
    96→        if re.search(r'!\w+', raw):
    97→            # But allow !!str, !!int etc (YAML builtin types)
    98→            lines = raw.split('\n')
    99→            for line in lines:
   100→                # Remove quoted strings
   101→                stripped = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '', line)
   102→                stripped = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", '', stripped)
   103→                # Check for custom tags (not !! builtin tags)
   104→                if re.search(r'(?<!!)!\w+', stripped):
   105→                    raise ParseError("YAML custom tags are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
   106→
   107→        result = yaml.load(raw, Loader=SafeYamlLoader)
   108→
   109→        # Check that all mapping keys are strings
   110→        def check_keys(obj):
   111→            if isinstance(obj, dict):
   112→                for k in obj.keys():
   113→                    if not isinstance(k, str):
   114→                        raise ParseError("YAML mapping keys must be strings", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
   115→                    check_keys(obj[k])
   116→            elif isinstance(obj, list):
   117→                for item in obj:
   118→                    check_keys(item)
   119→
   120→        check_keys(result)
   121→        return result
   122→    except yaml.YAMLError as e:
   123→        raise ParseError(f"Invalid YAML: {e}")
   124→
   125→
   126→def parse_toml(raw: str) -> Any:
   127→    """Parse a TOML string with strict rules (only JSON-representable values)."""
   128→    try:
   129→        result = toml.loads(raw)
   130→
   131→        # Check for non-JSON types
   132→        def check_json_types(obj, path=""):
   133→            if isinstance(obj, dict):
   134→                for k, v in obj.items():
   135→                    check_json_types(v, f"{path}/{k}")
   136→            elif isinstance(obj, list):
   137→                for i, item in enumerate(obj):
   138→                    check_json_types(item, f"{path}/{i}")
   139→            # Check for datetime objects
   140→            elif hasattr(obj, 'isoformat'):
   141→                raise ParseError(f"Non-JSON type at {path}: datetime not allowed", "non_json_type")
   142→
   143→        check_json_types(result)
   144→        return result
   145→    except toml.TomlDecodeError as e:
   146→        raise ParseError(f"Invalid TOML: {e}")
   147→
   148→
   149→def parse_raw_config(raw: str, fmt: str) -> Any:
   150→    fmt = fmt.lower()
   151→    parsers = {'json': parse_json, 'yaml': parse_yaml, 'toml': parse_toml}
   152→    if fmt not in parsers:
   153→        raise ParseError(f"Unsupported format: {fmt}")
   154→    return parsers[fmt](raw)
   155→
   156→
   157→class SchemaValidationError(Exception):
   158→    """Error during schema validation."""
   159→    def __init__(self, path: str, rule: str, expected: str, actual: str):
   160→        self.path = path
   161→        self.rule = rule
   162→        self.expected = expected
   163→        self.actual = actual
   164→        super().__init__(f"Validation failed at {path}")
   165→
   166→
   167→def get_json_type(value: Any) -> str:
   168→    """Get the JSON type of a value."""
   169→    if value is None:
   170→        return "null"
   171→    elif isinstance(value, bool):
   172→        return "boolean"
   173→    elif isinstance(value, int):
   174→        return "integer"
   175→    elif isinstance(value, float):
   176→        return "number"
   177→    elif isinstance(value, str):
   178→        return "string"
   179→    elif isinstance(value, list):
   180→        return "array"
   181→    elif isinstance(value, dict):
   182→        return "object"
   183→    return "unknown"
   184→
   185→
   186→class ExternalRefError(Exception):
   187→    pass
   188→
   189→
   190→def check_external_refs(obj):
   191→    """Raise ExternalRefError if obj contains any external $ref."""
   192→    if isinstance(obj, dict):
   193→        if '$ref' in obj:
   194→            ref = obj['$ref']
   195→            if ref.startswith(('http://', 'https://', '//')):
   196→                raise ExternalRefError()
   197→            if not ref.startswith('#') and ':' in ref.split('/')[0]:
   198→                raise ExternalRefError()
   199→        for v in obj.values():
   200→            check_external_refs(v)
   201→    elif isinstance(obj, list):
   202→        for item in obj:
   203→            check_external_refs(item)
   204→
   205→
   206→def validate_config_against_schema(config: Any, schema: Dict) -> None:
   207→    """Validate a config against a JSON Schema, raising SchemaValidationError on failure."""
   208→    try:
   209→        check_external_refs(schema)
   210→        validate(instance=config, schema=schema)
   211→    except jsonschema.exceptions.ValidationError as e:
   212→        # Extract path as JSON Pointer
   213→        path = "/" + "/".join(str(p) for p in e.absolute_path) if e.absolute_path else "/"
   214→
   215→        # Determine the rule and expected/actual values
   216→        rule = e.validator
   217→        expected = ""
   218→        actual = get_json_type(e.instance)
   219→
   220→        if rule == "type":
   221→            expected = e.validator_value
   222→            if isinstance(expected, list):
   223→                expected = ", ".join(expected)
   224→        elif rule == "enum":
   225→            expected = ", ".join(repr(v) for v in e.validator_value)
   226→        elif rule == "required":
   227→            path = path + "/" + e.validator_value[0] if e.validator_value else path
   228→            expected = "property required"
   229→            actual = "missing"
   230→        elif rule == "pattern":
   231→            expected = f"matching pattern {e.validator_value}"
   232→        elif rule == "minimum":
   233→            expected = f">= {e.validator_value}"
   234→            actual = str(e.instance)
   235→        elif rule == "maximum":
   236→            expected = f"<= {e.validator_value}"
   237→            actual = str(e.instance)
   238→        elif rule == "minLength":
   239→            expected = f"length >= {e.validator_value}"
   240→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   241→        elif rule == "maxLength":
   242→            expected = f"length <= {e.validator_value}"
   243→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   244→        elif rule == "minItems":
   245→            expected = f"items >= {e.validator_value}"
   246→            actual = f"items {len(e.instance)}"
   247→        elif rule == "maxItems":
   248→            expected = f"items <= {e.validator_value}"
   249→            actual = f"items {len(e.instance)}"
   250→        else:
   251→            expected = str(e.validator_value)
   252→            actual = str(e.instance)[:50]
   253→
   254→        raise SchemaValidationError(path, rule, expected, actual)
   255→    except ExternalRefError:
   256→        raise SchemaValidationError("/", "schema", "no external $ref", "external $ref found")
   257→    except Exception as e:
   258→        raise SchemaValidationError("/", "schema", "valid schema", str(e))
   259→
   260→
   261→class SchemaRegistry:
   262→    """Registry for JSON schemas with versioning."""
   263→
   264→    def __init__(self):
   265→        # schema_name -> {version -> schema}
   266→        self.schemas: Dict[str, Dict[int, Dict]] = {}
   267→
   268→    def create(self, name: str, schema: Dict) -> int:
   269→        """Create a new schema version, returning the version number."""
   270→        if name not in self.schemas:
   271→            self.schemas[name] = {}
   272→
   273→        versions = self.schemas[name]
   274→        if len(versions) >= 1000:
   275→            raise ValueError("Maximum schema versions exceeded")
   276→
   277→        new_version = max(versions.keys(), default=0) + 1
   278→        versions[new_version] = deepcopy(schema)
   279→        return new_version
   280→
   281→    def get(self, name: str, version: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
   282→        """Get a specific schema version."""
   283→        if name not in self.schemas:
   284→            return None
   285→        return self.schemas[name].get(version)
   286→
   287→    def list_versions(self, name: str) -> List[int]:
   288→        """List all versions of a schema."""
   289→        if name not in self.schemas:
   290→            return []
   291→        return sorted(self.schemas[name].keys())
   292→
   293→    def exists(self, name: str, version: int) -> bool:
   294→        """Check if a schema version exists."""
   295→        return name in self.schemas and version in self.schemas[name]
   296→
   297→
   298→class ConfigStore:
   299→    """Store for configurations with versioning."""
   300→
   301→    def __init__(self):
   302→        # (name, scope_key) -> {version -> config_entry}
   303→        self.configs: Dict[Tuple[str, str], Dict[int, Dict]] = {}
   304→        # (name, scope_key) -> binding_info
   305→        self.bindings: Dict[Tuple[str, str], Dict] = {}
   306→
   307→    @staticmethod
   308→    def scope_to_key(scope: Dict) -> str:
   309→        """Convert a scope dict to a canonical string key."""
   310→        return json.dumps(sorted(scope.items()))
   311→
   312→    @staticmethod
   313→    def key_to_scope(key: str) -> Dict:
   314→        """Convert a canonical string key back to a scope dict."""
   315→        return dict(json.loads(key))
   316→
   317→    def create_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict, config: Dict,
   318→                       includes: List[Dict] = None, schema_ref: Dict = None) -> int:
   319→        """Create a new config version."""
   320→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   321→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   322→
   323→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   324→            self.configs[full_key] = {}
   325→
   326→        versions = self.configs[full_key]
   327→        new_version = max(versions.keys(), default=0) + 1
   328→
   329→        entry = {
   330→            "config": normalize_value(deepcopy(config)),
   331→            "includes": includes or [],
   332→            "schema_ref": schema_ref
   333→        }
   334→        versions[new_version] = entry
   335→        return new_version
   336→
   337→    def get_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
   338→        """Get a specific config version."""
   339→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   340→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   341→
   342→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   343→            return None
   344→        return self.configs[full_key].get(version)
   345→
   346→    def get_latest_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict) -> Optional[int]:
   347→        """Get the latest version number for a config."""
   348→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   349→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   350→
   351→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   352→            return None
   353→        versions = self.configs[full_key]
   354→        if not versions:
   355→            return None
   356→        return max(versions.keys())
   357→
   358→    def get_config_entry(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int = None) -> Optional[Dict]:
   359→        """Get a config entry, optionally at a specific version."""
   360→        if version is None:
   361→            version = self.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   362→            if version is None:
   363→                return None
   364→        return self.get_version(name, scope, version)
   365→
   366→    def set_binding(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict) -> Dict:
   367→        """Set a schema binding for a config identity."""
   368→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   369→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   370→
   371→        binding = {
   372→            "name": name,
   373→            "scope": deepcopy(scope),
   374→            "schema_ref": deepcopy(schema_ref),
   375→            "active": True
   376→        }
   377→        self.bindings[full_key] = binding
   378→        return binding
   379→
   380→    def get_binding(self, name: str, scope: Dict) -> Optional[Dict]:
   381→        """Get the schema binding for a config identity."""
   382→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   383→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   384→        return self.bindings.get(full_key)
   385→
   386→    def resolve_config(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int = None,
   387→                       visited: set = None) -> Tuple[Dict, List[Dict]]:
   388→        """Resolve a config with includes, returning (resolved_config, inheritance_chain)."""
   389→        if visited is None:
   390→            visited = set()
   391→
   392→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   393→        visit_key = (name, scope_key, version)
   394→        if visit_key in visited:
   395→            raise ValueError(f"Circular include detected: {name}")
   396→        visited.add(visit_key)
   397→
   398→        entry = self.get_config_entry(name, scope, version)
   399→        if entry is None:
   400→            raise ValueError(f"Config not found: {name}")
   401→
   402→        config = deepcopy(entry["config"])
   403→        includes = entry.get("includes", [])
   404→
   405→        inheritance_chain = [{
   406→            "name": name,
   407→            "scope": deepcopy(scope),
   408→            "version": version or self.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   409→        }]
   410→
   411→        # Process includes in order (first one wins)
   412→        merged = {}
   413→        for inc in includes:
   414→            inc_name = inc["name"]
   415→            inc_scope = inc.get("scope", {})
   416→            inc_version = inc.get("version")
   417→
   418→            # Merge scopes: included config's scope + override from include directive
   419→            merged_scope = deepcopy(scope)
   420→            merged_scope.update(inc_scope)
   421→
   422→            inc_config, inc_chain = self.resolve_config(
   423→                inc_name, merged_scope, inc_version, visited.copy()
   424→            )
   425→            inheritance_chain.extend(inc_chain)
   426→
   427→            # Merge: included config values (lower priority)
   428→            merged = deep_merge(merged, inc_config)
   429→
   430→        # Current config values (higher priority)
   431→        merged = deep_merge(merged, config)
   432→
   433→        return merged, inheritance_chain
   434→
   435→
   436→def deep_merge(base: Dict, override: Dict) -> Dict:
   437→    """Deep merge two dictionaries, with override values taking precedence."""
   438→    result = deepcopy(base)
   439→    for key, value in override.items():
   440→        if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
   441→            result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
   442→        else:
   443→            result[key] = deepcopy(value)
   444→    return result
   445→
   446→
   447→class ConfigServerHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
   448→    """HTTP request handler for the config server."""
   449→
   450→    schemas: SchemaRegistry = None
   451→    configs: ConfigStore = None
   452→
   453→    def log_message(self, format, *args):
   454→        pass
   455→
   456→    def send_json_response(self, status: int, data: Any):
   457→        body = json.dumps(data, separators=(',', ':')).encode('utf-8')
   458→        self.send_response(status)
   459→        self.send_header('Content-Type', 'application/json; charset=utf-8')
   460→        self.send_header('Content-Length', len(body))
   461→        self.end_headers()
   462→        self.wfile.write(body)
   463→
   464→    def send_error_response(self, status: int, code: str, message: str, details: Dict = None):
   465→        error = {"code": code, "message": message}
   466→        if details:
   467→            error["details"] = details
   468→        self.send_json_response(status, {"error": error})
   469→
   470→    def send_validation_error(self, e: SchemaValidationError):
   471→        self.send_error_response(422, "validation_failed",
   472→                                "Config does not conform to schema", {
   473→                                    "path": e.path,
   474→                                    "rule": e.rule,
   475→                                    "expected": e.expected,
   476→                                    "actual": e.actual
   477→                                })
   478→
   479→    def resolve_effective_schema(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict = None):
   480→        """Return (effective_schema_ref, effective_schema) or (None, None) if sent error."""
   481→        if schema_ref:
   482→            schema_name = schema_ref.get("name")
   483→            schema_version = schema_ref.get("version")
   484→            if not self.schemas.exists(schema_name, schema_version):
   485→                self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Schema version not found")
   486→                return None, None
   487→            return schema_ref, self.schemas.get(schema_name, schema_version)
   488→        binding = self.configs.get_binding(name, scope)
   489→        if binding:
   490→            ref = binding["schema_ref"]
   491→            return ref, self.schemas.get(ref.get("name"), ref.get("version"))
   492→        return None, None
   493→
   494→    def read_request_body(self) -> bytes:
   495→        """Read the request body."""
   496→        content_length = int(self.headers.get('Content-Length', 0))
   497→        if content_length == 0:
   498→            return b'{}'
   499→
   500→        # Check size limit (1 MiB)
   501→        if content_length > 1024 * 1024:
   502→            return None
   503→
   504→        return self.rfile.read(content_length)
   505→
   506→    def parse_json_body(self) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[str]]:
   507→        """Parse the request body as JSON."""
   508→        body = self.read_request_body()
   509→        if body is None:
   510→            return None, "too_large"
   511→
   512→        try:
   513→            return json.loads(body.decode('utf-8')), None
   514→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   515→            return None, "invalid_json"
   516→
   517→    def parse_path(self) -> Tuple[str, List[str], Dict]:
   518→        """Parse the request path into components."""
   519→        parsed = urlparse(self.path)
   520→        path_parts = [p for p in parsed.path.split('/') if p]
   521→        query_params = parse_qs(parsed.query)
   522→        return parsed.path, path_parts, query_params
   523→
   524→    def do_POST(self):
   525→        """Handle POST requests."""
   526→        path, parts, query = self.parse_path()
   527→
   528→        # Validate content type
   529→        content_type = self.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
   530→        if not content_type.startswith('application/json'):
   531→            self.send_error_response(415, "unsupported_media_type",
   532→                                    "Content-Type must be application/json")
   533→            return
   534→
   535→        # Parse body
   536→        body, error = self.parse_json_body()
   537→        if error == "too_large":
   538→            self.send_error_response(413, "too_large", "Request body too large")
   539→            return
   540→        if error == "invalid_json":
   541→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid JSON body")
   542→            return
   543→
   544→        if body is None:
   545→            body = {}
   546→
   547→        # Route the request
   548→        try:
   549→            self.route_post(path, parts, body)
   550→        except Exception as e:
   551→            self.send_error_response(500, "internal_error", str(e))
   552→
   553→    def route_post(self, path: str, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   554→        if len(parts) < 3 or parts[0] != 'v1':
   555→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   556→            return
   557→
   558→        if parts[1] == 'schemas':
   559→            self._route_schema(parts, body)
   560→        elif parts[1] == 'configs':
   561→            self._route_config(parts, body)
   562→        else:
   563→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   564→
   565→    def _route_schema(self, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   566→        if len(parts) < 3:
   567→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   568→            return
   569→        schema_name = parts[2]
   570→
   571→        if len(parts) == 3:
   572→            self.handle_create_schema(schema_name, body)
   573→        elif len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == 'versions':
   574→            self.handle_list_schema_versions(schema_name, body)
   575→        elif len(parts) == 4:
   576→            try:
   577→                version = int(parts[3])
   578→                self.handle_get_schema_version(schema_name, version, body)
   579→            except ValueError:
   580→                self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid version number")
   581→        else:
   582→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   583→
   584→    def _route_config(self, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   585→        if len(parts) < 3:
   586→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   587→            return
   588→        name = parts[2]
   589→
   590→        # Handle action suffixes
   591→        if name.endswith(':bind'):
   592→            self.handle_bind_schema(name[:-5], body)
   593→        elif name.endswith(':resolve'):
   594→            self.handle_resolve_config(name[:-8], body)
   595→        elif name.endswith(':validate'):
   596→            self.handle_validate_config(name[:-9], body)
   597→        elif len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == 'schema':
   598→            self.handle_get_binding(name, body)
   599→        elif len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == 'versions':
   600→            self.handle_list_config_versions(name, body)
   601→        elif len(parts) == 4:
   602→            try:
   603→                version = int(parts[3])
   604→                self.handle_get_config_version(name, version, body)
   605→            except ValueError:
   606→                self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid version number")
   607→        elif len(parts) == 3:
   608→            self.handle_create_config(name, body)
   609→        else:
   610→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Endpoint not found")
   611→
   612→    def handle_create_schema(self, schema_name: str, body: Dict):
   613→        """Handle POST /v1/schemas/{schema_name}"""
   614→        schema = None
   615→
   616→        if "schema" in body:
   617→            # Structured JSON Schema
   618→            schema = body["schema"]
   619→        elif "raw_schema" in body:
   620→            # Raw schema
   621→            raw = body["raw_schema"]
   622→            fmt = body.get("raw_format", "json").lower()
   623→
   624→            if fmt not in ("json", "yaml"):
   625→                self.send_error_response(415, "unsupported_format",
   626→                                        f"Unsupported format: {fmt}")
   627→                return
   628→
   629→            # Check size
   630→            if len(raw.encode('utf-8')) > 1024 * 1024:
   631→                self.send_error_response(413, "too_large", "Schema too large")
   632→                return
   633→
   634→            try:
   635→                parsed = parse_raw_config(raw, fmt)
   636→                if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
   637→                    self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid",
   638→                                            "Schema must be an object")
   639→                    return
   640→                schema = parsed
   641→            except ParseError as e:
   642→                self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid", str(e),
   643→                                        {"reason": e.reason} if e.reason else None)
   644→                return
   645→        else:
   646→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request",
   647→                                    "Missing 'schema' or 'raw_schema' field")
   648→            return
   649→
   650→        # Validate it's a valid JSON Schema (basic check)
   651→        try:
   652→            check_external_refs(schema)
   653→            jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
   654→        except ExternalRefError:
   655→            self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid",
   656→                                    "External $ref not allowed",
   657→                                    {"reason": "external_ref_not_allowed"})
   658→            return
   659→        except jsonschema.exceptions.SchemaError as e:
   660→            self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid", str(e))
   661→            return
   662→        except Exception as e:
   663→            self.send_error_response(422, "schema_invalid", str(e))
   664→            return
   665→
   666→        # Create the schema version
   667→        try:
   668→            version = self.schemas.create(schema_name, schema)
   669→        except ValueError as e:
   670→            self.send_error_response(409, "conflict", str(e))
   671→            return
   672→
   673→        self.send_json_response(201, {"name": schema_name, "version": version})
   674→
   675→    def handle_list_schema_versions(self, schema_name: str, body: Dict):
   676→        """Handle POST /v1/schemas/{schema_name}/versions"""
   677→        versions = self.schemas.list_versions(schema_name)
   678→        self.send_json_response(200, {"name": schema_name, "versions": versions})
   679→
   680→    def handle_get_schema_version(self, schema_name: str, version: int, body: Dict):
   681→        """Handle POST /v1/schemas/{schema_name}/{version}"""
   682→        schema = self.schemas.get(schema_name, version)
   683→        if schema is None:
   684→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Schema version not found")
   685→            return
   686→
   687→        self.send_json_response(200, {
   688→            "name": schema_name,
   689→            "version": version,
   690→            "schema": normalize_value(schema)
   691→        })
   692→
   693→    def handle_bind_schema(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   694→        """Handle POST /v1/configs/{name}:bind"""
   695→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   696→        schema_ref = body.get("schema_ref")
   697→
   698→        if schema_ref is None:
   699→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Missing 'schema_ref'")
   700→            return
   701→
   702→        schema_name = schema_ref.get("name")
   703→        schema_version = schema_ref.get("version")
   704→
   705→        if not schema_name or schema_version is None:
   706→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid 'schema_ref'")
   707→            return
   708→
   709→        # Check schema exists
   710→        if not self.schemas.exists(schema_name, schema_version):
   711→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Schema version not found")
   712→            return
   713→
   714→        binding = self.configs.set_binding(name, scope, schema_ref)
   715→        self.send_json_response(200, binding)
   716→
   717→    def handle_get_binding(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   718→        """Handle POST /v1/configs/{name}/schema"""
   719→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   720→        binding = self.configs.get_binding(name, scope)
   721→
   722→        if binding is None:
   723→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "No binding found")
   724→            return
   725→
   726→        response = {
   727→            "name": binding["name"],
   728→            "scope": binding["scope"],
   729→            "schema_ref": binding["schema_ref"]
   730→        }
   731→        self.send_json_response(200, response)
   732→
   733→    def handle_create_config(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   734→        """Handle POST /v1/configs/{name}"""
   735→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   736→        includes = body.get("includes", [])
   737→        schema_ref = body.get("schema_ref")
   738→
   739→        config = None
   740→
   741→        if "config" in body:
   742→            # Structured JSON config
   743→            config = body["config"]
   744→        elif "raw_config" in body:
   745→            # Raw config
   746→            raw = body["raw_config"]
   747→            fmt = body.get("raw_format", "json").lower()
   748→
   749→            if fmt not in ("json", "yaml", "toml"):
   750→                self.send_error_response(415, "unsupported_format",
   751→                                        f"Unsupported format: {fmt}")
   752→                return
   753→
   754→            # Check size
   755→            if len(raw.encode('utf-8')) > 1024 * 1024:
   756→                self.send_error_response(413, "too_large", "Config too large")
   757→                return
   758→
   759→            try:
   760→                parsed = parse_raw_config(raw, fmt)
   761→                if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
   762→                    self.send_error_response(422, "unprocessable",
   763→                                            "Config must be a JSON object")
   764→                    return
   765→                config = parsed
   766→            except ParseError as e:
   767→                details = {"reason": e.reason} if e.reason else None
   768→                self.send_error_response(422, "unprocessable", str(e), details)
   769→                return
   770→        else:
   771→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request",
   772→                                    "Missing 'config' or 'raw_config' field")
   773→            return
   774→
   775→        # Normalize the config
   776→        config = normalize_value(config)
   777→
   778→        # Determine effective schema
   779→        effective_schema_ref, effective_schema = self.resolve_effective_schema(name, scope, schema_ref)
   780→        if effective_schema_ref is None and schema_ref is not None:
   781→            return  # Error already sent
   782→
   783→        # Validate against schema if present
   784→        if effective_schema:
   785→            try:
   786→                validate_config_against_schema(config, effective_schema)
   787→            except SchemaValidationError as e:
   788→                self.send_validation_error(e)
   789→                return
   790→
   791→        # Create the version
   792→        version = self.configs.create_version(name, scope, config, includes, schema_ref)
   793→
   794→        self.send_json_response(201, {
   795→            "name": name,
   796→            "scope": scope,
   797→            "version": version
   798→        })
   799→
   800→    def handle_list_config_versions(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   801→        """Handle POST /v1/configs/{name}/versions"""
   802→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   803→
   804→        scope_key = self.configs.scope_to_key(scope)
   805→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   806→
   807→        if full_key not in self.configs.configs:
   808→            self.send_json_response(200, {"name": name, "scope": scope, "versions": []})
   809→            return
   810→
   811→        versions = sorted(self.configs.configs[full_key].keys())
   812→        self.send_json_response(200, {"name": name, "scope": scope, "versions": versions})
   813→
   814→    def handle_get_config_version(self, name: str, version: int, body: Dict):
   815→        """Handle POST /v1/configs/{name}/{version}"""
   816→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   817→
   818→        entry = self.configs.get_version(name, scope, version)
   819→        if entry is None:
   820→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config version not found")
   821→            return
   822→
   823→        self.send_json_response(200, {
   824→            "name": name,
   825→            "scope": scope,
   826→            "version": version,
   827→            "config": entry["config"],
   828→            "includes": entry.get("includes", [])
   829→        })
   830→
   831→    def handle_resolve_config(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   832→        """Handle POST /v1/configs/{name}:resolve"""
   833→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   834→        version = body.get("version")
   835→        schema_ref = body.get("schema_ref")
   836→
   837→        # Check if config exists
   838→        latest = self.configs.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   839→        if latest is None:
   840→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config not found")
   841→            return
   842→
   843→        try:
   844→            resolved_config, inheritance_chain = self.configs.resolve_config(
   845→                name, scope, version
   846→            )
   847→        except ValueError as e:
   848→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", str(e))
   849→            return
   850→
   851→        # Determine effective schema
   852→        effective_schema_ref, effective_schema = self.resolve_effective_schema(name, scope, schema_ref)
   853→        if effective_schema_ref is None and schema_ref is not None:
   854→            return  # Error already sent
   855→
   856→        response = {
   857→            "name": name,
   858→            "scope": scope,
   859→            "resolved_config": normalize_value(resolved_config),
   860→            "inheritance_chain": inheritance_chain
   861→        }
   862→
   863→        if effective_schema:
   864→            try:
   865→                validate_config_against_schema(resolved_config, effective_schema)
   866→                response["validated_against"] = effective_schema_ref
   867→            except SchemaValidationError as e:
   868→                self.send_validation_error(e)
   869→                return
   870→
   871→        self.send_json_response(200, response)
   872→
   873→    def handle_validate_config(self, name: str, body: Dict):
   874→        scope = body.get("scope", {})
   875→        version = body.get("version")
   876→        schema_ref = body.get("schema_ref")
   877→        mode = body.get("mode", "resolved")
   878→
   879→        if mode not in ("stored", "resolved"):
   880→            self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", "Invalid mode")
   881→            return
   882→
   883→        latest = self.configs.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   884→        if latest is None:
   885→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config not found")
   886→            return
   887→
   888→        version_used = version or latest
   889→
   890→        entry = self.configs.get_version(name, scope, version_used)
   891→        if entry is None:
   892→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config version not found")
   893→            return
   894→
   895→        effective_schema_ref, effective_schema = self.resolve_effective_schema(name, scope, schema_ref)
   896→        if effective_schema_ref is None and schema_ref is not None:
   897→            return  # Error already sent
   898→
   899→        if effective_schema is None:
   900→            self.send_error_response(404, "schema_not_bound", "No schema bound")
   901→            return
   902→
   903→        if mode == "stored":
   904→            config_to_validate = entry["config"]
   905→        else:
   906→            try:
   907→                config_to_validate, _ = self.configs.resolve_config(name, scope, version_used)
   908→            except ValueError as e:
   909→                self.send_error_response(400, "bad_request", str(e))
   910→                return
   911→
   912→        try:
   913→            validate_config_against_schema(config_to_validate, effective_schema)
   914→            self.send_json_response(200, {
   915→                "name": name,
   916→                "scope": scope,
   917→                "version_used": version_used,
   918→                "mode": mode,
   919→                "valid": True,

    20→def normalize_value(value: Any) -> Any:
    21→    """Normalize a JSON value to canonical form with sorted keys."""
    22→    if isinstance(value, dict):
    23→        return {k: normalize_value(v) for k, v in sorted(value.items())}
    24→    if isinstance(value, list):
    25→        return [normalize_value(item) for item in value]
    26→    if isinstance(value, float) and value == int(value) and abs(value) < 10**15:
    27→        return float(int(value))
    28→    return value

    48→    pass

    60→SafeYamlLoader.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:null', lambda l, n: None)

    74→def parse_yaml(raw: str) -> Any:
    75→    """Parse a YAML string with strict rules."""
    76→    try:
    77→        if '<<:' in raw or '<< :' in raw:
    78→            raise ParseError("YAML merge keys are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    79→
    80→        if '&' in raw or '*' in raw:
    81→            lines = raw.split('\n')
    82→            for line in lines:
    83→                stripped = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '', line)
    84→                stripped = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", '', stripped)
    85→                if re.search(r'&\w+', stripped):
    86→                    raise ParseError("YAML anchors are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    87→                if re.search(r'\*\w+', stripped):
    88→                    raise ParseError("YAML aliases are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    89→
    90→        if re.search(r'!\w+', raw):
    91→            lines = raw.split('\n')
    92→            for line in lines:
    93→                stripped = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '', line)
    94→                stripped = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", '', stripped)
    95→                if re.search(r'(?<!!)!\w+', stripped):
    96→                    raise ParseError("YAML custom tags are not allowed", "yaml_feature_not_allowed")
    97→
    98→        result = yaml.load(raw, Loader=SafeYamlLoader)

   100→        def check_keys(obj):

   116→def parse_toml(raw: str) -> Any:
   117→    """Parse a TOML string with strict rules (only JSON-representable values)."""
   118→    try:
   119→        result = toml.loads(raw)
   120→
   121→        def check_json_types(obj, path=""):
   122→            if isinstance(obj, dict):
   123→                for k, v in obj.items():
   124→                    check_json_types(v, f"{path}/{k}")
   125→            elif isinstance(obj, list):
   126→                for i, item in enumerate(obj):
   127→                    check_json_types(item, f"{path}/{i}")
   128→            elif hasattr(obj, 'isoformat'):
   129→                raise ParseError(f"Non-JSON type at {path}: datetime not allowed", "non_json_type")
   130→
   131→        check_json_types(result)

   133→    except toml.TomlDecodeError as e:

   137→def parse_raw_config(raw: str, fmt: str) -> Any:
   138→    fmt = fmt.lower()
   139→    parsers = {'json': parse_json, 'yaml': parse_yaml, 'toml': parse_toml}
   140→    if fmt not in parsers:
   141→        raise ParseError(f"Unsupported format: {fmt}")
   142→    return parsers[fmt](raw)
