   145→class SchemaValidationError(Exception):
   146→    """Error during schema validation."""
   147→    def __init__(self, path: str, rule: str, expected: str, actual: str):
   148→        self.path = path
   149→        self.rule = rule
   150→        self.expected = expected
   151→        self.actual = actual
   152→        super().__init__(f"Validation failed at {path}")
   153→
   154→
   155→def get_json_type(value: Any) -> str:
   156→    """Get the JSON type of a value."""
   157→    if value is None:
   158→        return "null"
   159→    elif isinstance(value, bool):
   160→        return "boolean"
   161→    elif isinstance(value, int):
   162→        return "integer"
   163→    elif isinstance(value, float):
   164→        return "number"
   165→    elif isinstance(value, str):
   166→        return "string"
   167→    elif isinstance(value, list):
   168→        return "array"
   169→    elif isinstance(value, dict):
   170→        return "object"
   171→    return "unknown"
   172→
   173→
   174→class ExternalRefError(Exception):
   175→    pass
   176→
   177→
   178→def check_external_refs(obj):
   179→    """Raise ExternalRefError if obj contains any external $ref."""
   180→    if isinstance(obj, dict):
   181→        if '$ref' in obj:
   182→            ref = obj['$ref']
   183→            if ref.startswith(('http://', 'https://', '//')):
   184→                raise ExternalRefError()
   185→            if not ref.startswith('#') and ':' in ref.split('/')[0]:
   186→                raise ExternalRefError()
   187→        for v in obj.values():
   188→            check_external_refs(v)
   189→    elif isinstance(obj, list):
   190→        for item in obj:
   191→            check_external_refs(item)
   192→
   193→
   194→def validate_config_against_schema(config: Any, schema: Dict) -> None:
   195→    """Validate a config against a JSON Schema, raising SchemaValidationError on failure."""
   196→    try:
   197→        check_external_refs(schema)
   198→        validate(instance=config, schema=schema)
   199→    except jsonschema.exceptions.ValidationError as e:
   200→        # Extract path as JSON Pointer
   201→        path = "/" + "/".join(str(p) for p in e.absolute_path) if e.absolute_path else "/"
   202→        rule = e.validator
   203→        expected = ""
   204→        actual = get_json_type(e.instance)
   205→
   206→        if rule == "type":
   207→            expected = e.validator_value
   208→            if isinstance(expected, list):
   209→                expected = ", ".join(expected)
   210→        elif rule == "enum":
   211→            expected = ", ".join(repr(v) for v in e.validator_value)
   212→        elif rule == "required":
   213→            path = path + "/" + e.validator_value[0] if e.validator_value else path
   214→            expected = "property required"
   215→            actual = "missing"
   216→        elif rule == "pattern":
   217→            expected = f"matching pattern {e.validator_value}"
   218→        elif rule == "minimum":
   219→            expected = f">= {e.validator_value}"
   220→            actual = str(e.instance)
   221→        elif rule == "maximum":
   222→            expected = f"<= {e.validator_value}"
   223→            actual = str(e.instance)
   224→        elif rule == "minLength":
   225→            expected = f"length >= {e.validator_value}"
   226→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   227→        elif rule == "maxLength":
   228→            expected = f"length <= {e.validator_value}"
   229→            actual = f"length {len(e.instance)}"
   230→        elif rule == "minItems":
   231→            expected = f"items >= {e.validator_value}"
   232→            actual = f"items {len(e.instance)}"
   233→        elif rule == "maxItems":
   234→            expected = f"items <= {e.validator_value}"
   235→            actual = f"items {len(e.instance)}"
   236→        else:
   237→            expected = str(e.validator_value)
   238→            actual = str(e.instance)[:50]
   239→
   240→        raise SchemaValidationError(path, rule, expected, actual)
   241→    except ExternalRefError:
   242→        raise SchemaValidationError("/", "schema", "no external $ref", "external $ref found")
   243→    except Exception as e:
   244→        raise SchemaValidationError("/", "schema", "valid schema", str(e))

   247→class SchemaRegistry:
   248→    """Registry for JSON schemas with versioning."""
   249→
   250→    def __init__(self):
   251→        self.schemas: Dict[str, Dict[int, Dict]] = {}
   252→
   253→    def create(self, name: str, schema: Dict) -> int:
   254→        """Create a new schema version, returning the version number."""
   255→        if name not in self.schemas:
   256→            self.schemas[name] = {}
   257→
   258→        versions = self.schemas[name]
   259→        if len(versions) >= 1000:
   260→            raise ValueError("Maximum schema versions exceeded")
   261→
   262→        new_version = max(versions.keys(), default=0) + 1
   263→        versions[new_version] = deepcopy(schema)
   264→        return new_version
   265→
   266→    def get(self, name: str, version: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
   267→        """Get a specific schema version."""
   268→        if name not in self.schemas:
   269→            return None
   270→        return self.schemas[name].get(version)
   271→
   272→    def list_versions(self, name: str) -> List[int]:
   273→        """List all versions of a schema."""
   274→        if name not in self.schemas:
   275→            return []
   276→        return sorted(self.schemas[name].keys())
   277→
   278→    def exists(self, name: str, version: int) -> bool:
   279→        """Check if a schema version exists."""
   280→        return name in self.schemas and version in self.schemas[name]
   281→
   282→
   283→class ConfigStore:
   284→    """Store for configurations with versioning."""
   285→
   286→    def __init__(self):
   287→        self.configs: Dict[Tuple[str, str], Dict[int, Dict]] = {}
   288→        self.bindings: Dict[Tuple[str, str], Dict] = {}
   289→
   290→    @staticmethod
   291→    def scope_to_key(scope: Dict) -> str:
   292→        """Convert a scope dict to a canonical string key."""
   293→        return json.dumps(sorted(scope.items()))
   294→
   295→    @staticmethod
   296→    def key_to_scope(key: str) -> Dict:
   297→        """Convert a canonical string key back to a scope dict."""
   298→        return dict(json.loads(key))
   299→
   300→    def create_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict, config: Dict,
   301→                       includes: List[Dict] = None, schema_ref: Dict = None) -> int:
   302→        """Create a new config version."""
   303→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   304→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   305→
   306→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   307→            self.configs[full_key] = {}
   308→
   309→        versions = self.configs[full_key]
   310→        new_version = max(versions.keys(), default=0) + 1
   311→
   312→        entry = {
   313→            "config": normalize_value(deepcopy(config)),
   314→            "includes": includes or [],
   315→            "schema_ref": schema_ref
   316→        }
   317→        versions[new_version] = entry
   318→        return new_version
   319→
   320→    def get_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
   321→        """Get a specific config version."""
   322→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   323→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   324→
   325→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   326→            return None
   327→        return self.configs[full_key].get(version)
   328→
   329→    def get_latest_version(self, name: str, scope: Dict) -> Optional[int]:
   330→        """Get the latest version number for a config."""
   331→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   332→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   333→
   334→        if full_key not in self.configs:
   335→            return None
   336→        versions = self.configs[full_key]
   337→        if not versions:
   338→            return None
   339→        return max(versions.keys())
   340→
   341→    def get_config_entry(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int = None) -> Optional[Dict]:
   342→        """Get a config entry, optionally at a specific version."""
   343→        if version is None:
   344→            version = self.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   345→            if version is None:
   346→                return None
   347→        return self.get_version(name, scope, version)
   348→
   349→    def set_binding(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict) -> Dict:
   350→        """Set a schema binding for a config identity."""
   351→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   352→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   353→
   354→        binding = {
   355→            "name": name,
   356→            "scope": deepcopy(scope),
   357→            "schema_ref": deepcopy(schema_ref),
   358→            "active": True
   359→        }
   360→        self.bindings[full_key] = binding
   361→        return binding
   362→
   363→    def get_binding(self, name: str, scope: Dict) -> Optional[Dict]:
   364→        """Get the schema binding for a config identity."""
   365→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   366→        full_key = (name, scope_key)
   367→        return self.bindings.get(full_key)
   368→
   369→    def resolve_config(self, name: str, scope: Dict, version: int = None,
   370→                       visited: set = None) -> Tuple[Dict, List[Dict]]:
   371→        """Resolve a config with includes, returning (resolved_config, inheritance_chain)."""
   372→        if visited is None:
   373→            visited = set()
   374→
   375→        scope_key = self.scope_to_key(scope)
   376→        visit_key = (name, scope_key, version)
   377→        if visit_key in visited:
   378→            raise ValueError(f"Circular include detected: {name}")
   379→        visited.add(visit_key)
   380→
   381→        entry = self.get_config_entry(name, scope, version)
   382→        if entry is None:
   383→            raise ValueError(f"Config not found: {name}")
   384→
   385→        config = deepcopy(entry["config"])
   386→        includes = entry.get("includes", [])
   387→
   388→        inheritance_chain = [{
   389→            "name": name,
   390→            "scope": deepcopy(scope),
   391→            "version": version or self.get_latest_version(name, scope)
   392→        }]
   393→
   394→        merged = {}
   395→        for inc in includes:
   396→            inc_name = inc["name"]
   397→            inc_scope = inc.get("scope", {})
   398→            inc_version = inc.get("version")
   399→
   400→            merged_scope = deepcopy(scope)
   401→            merged_scope.update(inc_scope)
   402→
   403→            inc_config, inc_chain = self.resolve_config(
   404→                inc_name, merged_scope, inc_version, visited.copy()
   405→            )
   406→            inheritance_chain.extend(inc_chain)
   407→
   408→            merged = deep_merge(merged, inc_config)
   409→
   410→        merged = deep_merge(merged, config)
   411→
   412→        return merged, inheritance_chain
   413→
   414→
   415→def deep_merge(base: Dict, override: Dict) -> Dict:
   416→    """Deep merge two dictionaries, with override values taking precedence."""
   417→    result = deepcopy(base)
   418→    for key, value in override.items():
   419→        if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
   420→            result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
   421→        else:
   422→            result[key] = deepcopy(value)
   423→    return result

   426→class ConfigServerHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
   427→    """HTTP request handler for the config server."""
   428→
   429→    schemas: SchemaRegistry = None
   430→    configs: ConfigStore = None
   431→
   432→    def log_message(self, format, *args):
   433→        pass
   434→
   435→    def send_json_response(self, status: int, data: Any):
   436→        body = json.dumps(data, separators=(',', ':')).encode('utf-8')
   437→        self.send_response(status)
   438→        self.send_header('Content-Type', 'application/json; charset=utf-8')
   439→        self.send_header('Content-Length', len(body))
   440→        self.end_headers()
   441→        self.wfile.write(body)
   442→
   443→    def send_error_response(self, status: int, code: str, message: str, details: Dict = None):
   444→        error = {"code": code, "message": message}
   445→        if details:
   446→            error["details"] = details
   447→        self.send_json_response(status, {"error": error})
   448→
   449→    def send_validation_error(self, e: SchemaValidationError):
   450→        self.send_error_response(422, "validation_failed",
   451→                                "Config does not conform to schema", {
   452→                                    "path": e.path,
   453→                                    "rule": e.rule,
   454→                                    "expected": e.expected,
   455→                                    "actual": e.actual
   456→                                })
   457→
   458→    def resolve_effective_schema(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict = None):
   459→        """Return (effective_schema_ref, effective_schema) or (None, None) if sent error."""
   460→        if schema_ref:
   461→            schema_name = schema_ref.get("name")
   462→            schema_version = schema_ref.get("version")
   463→            if not self.schemas.exists(schema_name, schema_version):

   473→    def read_request_body(self) -> bytes:

   483→    def parse_json_body(self) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[str]]:

   526→    def route_post(self, path: str, parts: List[str], body: Dict):
   527→        if len(parts) < 3 or parts[0] != 'v1':

   538→    def _route_schema(self, parts: List[str], body: Dict):

   557→    def _route_config(self, parts: List[str], body: Dict):

   584→    def handle_create_schema(self, schema_name: str, body: Dict):

   759→    def handle_list_config_versions(self, name: str, body: Dict):

   790→    def handle_resolve_config(self, name: str, body: Dict):

   795→
   796→        latest = self.configs.get_latest_version(name, scope)

   830→    def handle_validate_config(self, name: str, body: Dict):

   842→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config not found")
   843→            return
   844→
   845→        version_used = version or latest
   846→
   847→        entry = self.configs.get_version(name, scope, version_used)
   848→        if entry is None:
   849→            self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Config version not found")
   850→            return

   853→        if effective_schema_ref is None and schema_ref is not None:

   858→            return

   867→                return

   874→                "version_used": version_used,
   875→                "mode": mode,
   876→                "valid": True,
   877→                "validated_against": effective_schema_ref
   878→            })
   879→        except SchemaValidationError as e:
   880→            self.send_validation_error(e)

   883→def run_server(address: str = "0.0.0.0", port: int = 8080):
   884→    ConfigServerHandler.schemas = SchemaRegistry()
   885→    ConfigServerHandler.configs = ConfigStore()
   886→
   887→    server = HTTPServer((address, port), ConfigServerHandler)
   888→    print(f"Config server running on {address}:{port}", file=sys.stderr)
   889→
   890→    try:
   891→        server.serve_forever()
   892→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   893→        print("\nShutting down...", file=sys.stderr)
   894→        server.shutdown()
   895→
   896→
   897→def main():
   898→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Config Server")
   899→    parser.add_argument("--address", default="0.0.0.0", help="Address to bind to")
   900→    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8080, help="Port to bind to")
   901→    args = parser.parse_args()

     def resolve_effective_schema(self, name: str, scope: Dict, schema_ref: Dict = None):
         """Return (effective_schema_ref, effective_schema) or (None, None) if sent error."""
         if schema_ref:
             schema_name = schema_ref.get("name")
             schema_version = schema_ref.get("version")
             if not self.schemas.exists(schema_name, schema_version):
                 self.send_error_response(404, "not_found", "Schema version not found")
                 return None, None
             return schema_ref, self.schemas.get(schema_name, schema_version)
         binding = self.configs.get_binding(name, scope)
         if binding:
             ref = binding["schema_ref"]
             return ref, self.schemas.get(ref.get("name"), ref.get("version"))
         return None, None
